title: Recruiter Onboarding Guide
paragraphs: Client procurement, ticket standards, quota rules, and payment process

title: 1. Agency overview
paragraphs: Shrouded Development is a Roblox development agency that connects clients with skilled developers for paid work. The agency helps with client procurement, developer matching, ticket organization, communication, and transactions.
Services include scripting, building, modelling, animation, graphics, UI/UX, VFX, SFX, and project management.

title: 2. Recruiter role
paragraphs: As a Recruiter, your role is to find clients who need Roblox development services and bring qualified leads into Shrouded. You are responsible for the first impression, the first conversation, and making sure the client understands what Shrouded can provide.
You are not the client manager. Once a ticket is open, the Agent leads the commission.

title: Send relevant developer portfolios when useful
paragraphs: Filter low value offers and percentage only offers
Make sure the client understands our minimum prices

title: Invite interested clients to the Discord server
paragraphs: Ask interested clients to open a commission ticket
Track the ticket so you know when to request your recruiter payment

title: 4. Client outreach standard
paragraphs: Every message should feel written for that client, not copied into their inbox. Mention what they asked for, send portfolios that fit their request, and keep your tone direct and respectful.

title: 3. Send a short tailored message and relevant portfolio examples.
paragraphs: 4. Answer simple questions about services, minimum pricing, and payment methods.
5. If they are interested, invite them to the Discord server and ask them to open a ticket.

title: 5. After a client says yes
paragraphs: Once a client is interested, your job shifts from outreach to handoff.

title: 4. Stay in the ticket only to track status and payment progress.
paragraphs: 5. Request your recruiter payment only after the qualifying payment has proceeded.
The client may be interested and still not hire our developers. Interest alone does not qualify for recruiter payment.

title: 6. Ticket behavior
paragraphs: Recruiters are usually added to client tickets so they can track the commission. You are not added to run the ticket.
Do not talk in tickets unless strictly needed.
Do not interfere with Agent communication.
Do not process payments yourself.
Do not share screenshots, files, or private ticket information.
Do not make promises outside Shrouded policy.
If you must speak, keep it short and professional.

title: 7. Pricing minimums
paragraphs: Recruiters must filter offers before inviting clients. Do not bring in jobs that are below the minimum or based only on percentages.
Prices can increase depending on complexity, deadline, developer role, and project size.

title: 8. Payment methods and upfront payment
paragraphs: Shrouded accepts PayPal USD, Crypto USD, and Robux. Recruiters may request to receive their payment through one form only, but fees or conversion differences may slightly reduce the received amount.
Shrouded requires 50 percent upfront payment before a developer starts work. This protects the agency and the developer if a client backs out later.
Transactions are handled by the Agent. Recruiters track payment status but do not process transactions.

title: 9. Recruiter payment
paragraphs: Recruiter payment is based on the total commission value and is requested once the client proceeds with the qualifying payment.

title: 10. Payment request format
paragraphs: Use the format below when requesting your cut.

title: 11. Quota and strikes
paragraphs: Recruiters are expected to bring in commissions every week. Current active quota is two commissions or $100 worth of commission value per week unless leadership announces a different target.
A commission counts toward quota only when the client opens a ticket and proceeds with payment.
Failure to meet quota may result in a strike. Three strikes can result in removal. For Senior Recruiters, three strikes can result in demotion. Strikes expire after two months.

title: 12. Clients Shrouded does not take
paragraphs: Clients looking to hire developers for their own studios

title: Clients who act disrespectfully or suspiciously
paragraphs: If you are unsure whether a client should be accepted, ask staff before inviting them.

title: 13. Server roles
paragraphs: Recruiters should understand who handles each part of Shrouded work.

title: 14. Competitions and promotions
paragraphs: Shrouded may hold monthly recruiter competitions. Competitions can be separated between Recruiters and Senior Recruiters.
A top performing Recruiter may be promoted to Senior Recruiter for the following month. Senior Recruiter competitions may include separate rewards.

title: 15. Final checklist
paragraphs: Questions may go to meowwalice on Discord or to the appropriate Shrouded staff member.
